summary: ollama-helpers postinstall harvests developer identity and exfiltrates to anonymous Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-owned identity files (~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, ~/.config/gh/hosts.yaml) to extract the developer's git email, GitHub login, and GitHub email, then collects machine hostname, OS username, current working directory, OS platform, and CI provider, and HTTPS-POSTs a JSON body containing all of this to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. A header comment falsely claims the data is 'anonymous' and 'one-way hashed', but the code transmits plaintext PII. The destination is an anonymous Google Cloud Run endpoint that does not match the package's declared homepage (ollama-js.dev). The package's declared `main: dist/index.js` is absent from the tarball, so the install-time exfiltration is effectively the package's only behavior. The name and metadata position this as a companion to the legitimate `ollama` package, but no such relationship exists.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
